text:
The intermediate step is to calculate the product of the base and the height, which is \(8 \times 5 = 40\).

The area of the triangle is given by the formula \(\frac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times \text{height}\). Using the intermediate value, the area is \(\frac{1}{2} \times 40 = 20\).

So, the final answer is \(20\).
